title: senecio integerrimus var. vaseyi
intro: Senecio integerrimus var. vaseyi, also known as Vasey's ragwort, is a rare perennial plant species in the daisy family that is native to the western United States. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, which have been found to be beneficial in treating various health issues such as arthritis, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Senecio integerrimus var. vaseyi include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, promote wound healing, and ease respiratory congestion, among others. Senecio integerrimus var. vaseyi is typically prepared for medicinal use through infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be made by steeping the plant's leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
